name: Tour of the Natural Park of Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán
name_original: Vuelta por el Parque Natural de la Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán
region: National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán
region_intro: Touring cycling around National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán offers diverse landscapes west of Cartagena in the Murcia region of Spain. The area features a dramatic "great stone mass" rising from the Mediterranean Sea, with mountain ranges and coastal heads plunging into the sea, creating cliffs and secluded coves. Terrestrial environments range from Mediterranean forests with pine trees to more arid areas with palm trees and esparto grass. The park's geology is primarily limestone, with some metamorphic and volcanic rocks. Best touring cycling routes around National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán The most popular touring cycling route is Tour of the Natural Park of Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán, a difficult 23.0 miles (37.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete, exploring the park's varied natural features. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cueva del Agua – The Gredas of Bolnuevo loop from La Chapineta, a moderate 21.3 miles (34.3 km) path, offering views of unique geological formations and coastal scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Territorio Tortuga Mural – Águilas Lighthouse loop from Los Madriles, Camping, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through coastal paths and local landmarks, often completed in about 50 minutes. Touring cycling around National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán is defined by coastal paths, mountain ranges, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging expeditions. The routes in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 7,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: There are over 250 touring cycling routes available in the National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán. These routes cater to a wide range of abilities, from easy rides to more challenging expeditions, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What are the typical terrain and elevation changes like for touring cycling in this region?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll encounter dramatic coastal paths with steep slopes plunging into the sea, as well as routes through Mediterranean forests and more arid areas. Elevation gains can vary significantly; easy rides might have around 500 feet, while harder expeditions can involve over 1500 feet of climbing, sometimes with unpaved segments.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: The National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán is generally pleasant for cycling outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn offer ideal conditions with milder temperatures, making it comfortable to explore both coastal and inland routes. Winters are also generally mild, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy breathtaking natural features and viewpoints. A notable stop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3067287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedacero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of La Azohía and Isla Plana. You might also encounter the refreshing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1462793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Cañar&lt;/a&gt;, a natural spring, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6581220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the sierra.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. A prominent landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batería de Castillitos&lt;/a&gt; on Cabo Tiñoso, a historic military fortification from the 1930s that resembles a medieval castle and offers incredible bay views. The path to it is relatively flat, making it accessible for cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 1,200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from stunning coastal views to tranquil Mediterranean forests, and the variety of routes catering to different fitness levels. The blend of natural beauty and historical sites is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for all ages?
A: Yes, the park offers several easy touring cycling routes that are suitable for families. These routes are typically shorter, under two hours, with minimal elevation gain (around 500 feet) and often on paved surfaces, making them accessible for various fitness levels. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Territorio Tortuga Mural – Águilas Lighthouse loop from Los Madriles, Camping&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 miles and takes about 50 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3967959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua – The Gredas of Bolnuevo loop from La Chapineta&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 21.3-mile journey with unique geological formations. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40363167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert path with palm trees – Moreras Mountain Range loop from Los Madriles, Camping&lt;/a&gt;, a 50.7 km route.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in the National Park Sierra de la Muela?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, many routes, particularly those starting from towns or camping areas like Los Madriles, Camping, will have designated parking facilities nearby. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking information.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants near the touring cycling trails for a break?
A: Yes, the region has various towns and coastal villages, such as La Azohía and Isla Plana, where you can find cafes and restaurants to refuel during or after your ride. These spots often offer local cuisine and refreshing drinks, perfect for a break.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For very fit and experienced cyclists, there are challenging routes exceeding five hours or involving over 1500 feet of elevation gain, with some segments that may require pushing your bike. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016737280/tour-of-the-natural-park-of-sierra-de-la-muela-cabo-tinoso-and-roldan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Natural Park of Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 23.0-mile trail with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall of Mazarrón – Mazarrón Mines Historic Site loop from Los Madriles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in the National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: The National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán is located west of Cartagena in the Murcia region. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads are not detailed, major towns in the vicinity, like Cartagena, will have bus services. Cyclists often drive to a starting point or use local taxi services that can accommodate bikes to access the routes.
tips: There are beautiful views of La Azohía and Isla Plana from the Mirador del Cedacero. If you cycle through the area it is a mandatory stop.

This regional park stands out for its wonderful gorges, steep hills and tiny coves with turquoise water. In addition, there are numerous roads and paths that go into the heart of the mountains, where you can visit the ruins of dismantled batteries, caves and watchtowers.

Very very nice. We were here in May and everything was blooming, colorful and just beautiful.
Motorists proved to be very tolerant. So definitely get on the bike. 🚴👍
